ddls:
CREATE TABLE "memory_addresses" (
  "address_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each memory address request */,
  "address_hex" TEXT /* Hexadecimal representation of the memory address */,
  "tag_bits" TEXT /* Binary representation of the tag bits extracted from the address */,
  "index_bits" INTEGER /* Binary representation of the index bits extracted from the address */,
  "block_offset_bits" INTEGER /* Binary representation of the block offset bits extracted from the address */,
  "request_time" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the memory address was requested */,
  "request_type" TEXT /* Type of request (e.g., read, write) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("address_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "cache_blocks" (
  "block_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each cache block */,
  "cache_level" INTEGER /* Level of the cache where the block resides */,
  "valid_bit" INTEGER /* Indicates if the block contains valid data */,
  "tag" TEXT /* Tag bits associated with the block */,
  "data" TEXT /* Raw data stored in the cache block */,
  "dirty_bit" INTEGER /* Indicates if the data in the block has been modified */,
  "last_access_time" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last access to the block */,
  "replacement_policy_id" INTEGER /* ID of the replacement policy used for this block */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("block_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_cache_blocks_cache_level FOREIGN KEY ("cache_level") REFERENCES cache_levels ("level_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_cache_blocks_replacement_policy_id FOREIGN KEY ("replacement_policy_id") REFERENCES replacement_policies ("policy_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "cache_levels" (
  "level_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each cache level */,
  "level_number" INTEGER /* Number of the cache level (e.g., L1, L2) */,
  "size" INTEGER /* Size of the cache level in bytes */,
  "associativity" INTEGER /* Associativity of the cache level */,
  "line_size" INTEGER /* Size of each cache line in bytes */,
  "replacement_policy_id" INTEGER /* ID of the default replacement policy for this cache level */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("level_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_cache_levels_replacement_policy_id FOREIGN KEY ("replacement_policy_id") REFERENCES replacement_policies ("policy_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "cache_hits" (
  "hit_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each cache hit event */,
  "address_id" INTEGER /* ID of the memory address that resulted in a hit */,
  "block_id" INTEGER /* ID of the cache block that was hit */,
  "hit_time" REAL /* Time taken to access the cache block */,
  "hit_type" TEXT /* Type of hit (e.g., read hit, write hit) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("hit_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_cache_hits_block_id FOREIGN KEY ("block_id") REFERENCES cache_blocks ("block_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "cache_misses" (
  "miss_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each cache miss event */,
  "address_id" INTEGER /* ID of the memory address that resulted in a miss */,
  "reason" TEXT /* Reason for the cache miss */,
  "miss_time" REAL /* Time taken to handle the cache miss */,
  "miss_type" TEXT /* Type of miss (e.g., compulsory miss, capacity miss, conflict miss) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("miss_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "prefetching" (
  "prefetch_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each prefetching operation */,
  "address_id" INTEGER /* ID of the original memory address that triggered the prefetch */,
  "predicted_address" TEXT /* Predicted memory address that was prefetched */,
  "prefetch_time" REAL /* Time taken to perform the prefetch operation */,
  "prefetch_type" TEXT /* Type of prefetch (e.g., data prefetch, instruction prefetch) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("prefetch_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_prefetching_address_id FOREIGN KEY ("address_id") REFERENCES memory_addresses ("address_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "replacement_policies" (
  "policy_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each replacement policy */,
  "policy_name" TEXT /* Name of the cache replacement policy */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of how the policy works */,
  "policy_type" TEXT /* Type of policy (e.g., eviction, allocation) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("policy_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "cache_performance" (
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each performance metric */,
  "metric_name" TEXT /* Name of the performance metric (e.g., hit rate, miss rate) */,
  "value" REAL /* Value of the performance metric */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp when the metric was recorded */,
  "cache_level_id" INTEGER /* ID of the cache level for which the metric is recorded */,
  "metric_type" TEXT /* Type of metric (e.g., hit rate, miss rate, average access time) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("metric_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "system_simulations" (
  "simulation_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each simulation */,
  "start_time" TEXT /* Start time of the simulation */,
  "end_time" TEXT /* End time of the simulation */,
  "cache_configuration" TEXT /* Configuration of the cache used in the simulation */,
  "performance_metrics" TEXT /* Performance metrics recorded during the simulation */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who performed the simulation */,
  "simulation_type" TEXT /* Type of simulation (e.g., single-level, multi-level) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("simulation_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_system_simulations_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., engineer, researcher) */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last login */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department of the user (e.g., engineering, research) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "simulation_reports" (
  "report_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each report */,
  "simulation_id" INTEGER /* ID of the simulation that generated the report */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who generated the report */,
  "report_content" TEXT /* Content of the report */,
  "report_time" TEXT /* Time when the report was generated */,
  "report_type" TEXT /* Type of report (e.g., performance, configuration) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("report_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_simulation_reports_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
